subclassFluff.name: Path of Lament
entries: {@i Transform Bitter Anguish into Supernatural Rage}

subclassFluff.name: Primordial Patron
entries: {@i Invoke Scions of Elemental Chaos}

subclassFluff.name: Warrior of Venom
entries: {@i Channel the Cauldron of Your Own Toxicity}

subclassFeature.name: Path of Lament
entries: Barbarians who walk the Path of Lament hone their regrets into deadly weapons and channel their deepest sorrows into rageful action. Propelled by supernatural grief, their fury grants them gifts from beyond the grave.

A Barbarian may choose the Path of Lament, but more often the path is thrust on the Barbarian by unhappy accident. At the heart of the Barbarian's rage are great trauma and unresolved grief—painful emotions fueled by haunting memories that rise to the surface during adrenaline-filled moments. You can roll on or choose a result from the {@table Path of Lament Origins|XUA2026VillainousOptions2} table to inspire the incident that forms the root of your despair.

subclassFeature.name: Banshee's Wail
entries: When you activate your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|XPHB|1|XPHB} or as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|XPHB|1|XPHB} is active, you can let out a doleful wail. Each creature of your choice in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you makes a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Constitution modifier and {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failed save, a creature takes Psychic damage and has the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only. To determine the Psychic damage, roll a number of {@dice d12|d12s} equal to your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|XPHB|1|XPHB|Rage Damage} bonus, and add them together.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also regain all uses by expending a use of your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|XPHB|1|XPHB} (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Commune with the Dead
entries: You can cast the {@spell Speak with Dead|XPHB} spell but only as a {@variantrule Ritual|XPHB}. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

subclassFeature.name: Horrifying Strike
entries: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a Strength-based attack roll while your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|XPHB|1|XPHB} is active, you can attempt to horrify the target. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Constitution modifier and {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Otherworldly Anguish
entries: You draw power from a sorrow so deep it extends beyond the boundaries of the realm of the living. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Deathly Wail
entries: If a target fails its saving throw against your {@subclassFeature Banshee's Wail|Barbarian|XPHB|Lament|XUA2026VillainousOptions2|3|XUA2026VillainousOptions2} and it has {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice your {@class Barbarian|XPHB} level or fewer, it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} instead of taking damage.

entries.name: Impenetrable Sorrow
entries: You can't be {@variantrule Possession|XPHB|possessed}.

entries.name: Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold and Necrotic damage while your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|XPHB|1|XPHB} is active.

subclassFeature.name: Sorrow Form
entries: When you activate your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|XPHB|1|XPHB}, you can empower yourself with undeath. You gain the benefits below for 1 minute or until you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Immunities
entries: You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions. If you're {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} when you empower yourself, the condition ends on you. In addition, you can't gain {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels.

entries.name: Life-Draining Strike
entries: When a creature fails its saving throw against your {@subclassFeature Horrifying Strike|Barbarian|XPHB|Lament|XUA2026VillainousOptions2|6|XUA2026VillainousOptions2}, the creature takes {@damage 2d10} Necrotic damage. You regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Necrotic damage dealt.

entries.name: Undead
entries: Your creature type is Undead.

subclassFeature.name: Warrior of Venom
entries: Warriors of Venom pollute their internal reservoirs of power to become poison incarnate. Through years of focus and the study of poison in all its forms, these Monks learn to harness their own toxicity to impair and envenom foes. Contact with a Warrior of Venom—even the slightest touch or a single drop of blood—can be as deadly as a viper's bite.

subclassFeature.name: Envenom Weapon
entries: At the start of your turn, you can expend 1 Focus Point to apply a toxin produced from your blood to one Monk weapon that you're holding. A creature that takes damage from the weapon is subjected to one of the following toxin effects (choose when you apply the toxin):

items.name: Slowing Toxin
entries: Until the start of your next turn, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved; it can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions}; and it can take either an action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on its turn, not both.

items.name: Venom
entries: The target takes Poison damage equal to two rolls of your {@classFeature Martial Arts|Monk|XPHB|1|XPHB} die.
The toxin retains potency for 1 minute or until a creature takes damage from the weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Arsenal
entries: You gain a {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}, and you have proficiency with it. When creating a {@item Basic Poison|XPHB}, you can do so over the course of 1 day (8 hours of work). Additionally, whenever you deal Poison damage with a {@class Monk|XPHB} feature or a Monk weapon, you can change that damage type to Acid.

subclassFeature.name: Toxic Touch
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 Focus Point to apply a potent toxin to a creature you touch. The target makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.
While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, the target is affected by one of the following effects of your choice:

items.name: Intoxicant
entries: The target has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for the duration or until you or your allies deal damage to the target.

items.name: Sedative
entries: The creature falls asleep and has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition for the duration. Another creature can use an action to shake it awake and remove the condition.

items.name: Truth Serum
entries: The target can't knowingly communicate a lie for the duration.

subclassFeature.name: Toxic Blood
entries: Enemies draw your toxic blood at their own peril. Whenever a creature hits you with a melee attack roll, the attacker takes {@damage 1d6} Poison damage. If you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, the attacker instead takes Poison damage equal to one roll of your {@classFeature Martial Arts|Monk|XPHB|1|XPHB} die.

subclassFeature.name: Toxin Refiner
entries: Your body can filter poison. You gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison damage. Whenever you are subjected to Poison damage, your {@subclassFeature Envenom Weapon|Monk|XPHB|Venom|XUA2026VillainousOptions2|3|XUA2026VillainousOptions2} options each deal extra Poison damage equal to one roll of your {@classFeature Martial Arts|Monk|XPHB|1|XPHB} die, and you can't gain this benefit again until the end of your next turn. In addition, whenever you ingest a poison, you regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to one roll of your {@classFeature Martial Arts|Monk|XPHB|1|XPHB} die.

subclassFeature.name: Hallucinogenic Breath
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can expend 2 Focus Points and replace one of your attacks with an exhalation of hallucinogenic vapors at one creature you can see within 30 feet. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes Poison damage equal to three rolls of your {@classFeature Martial Arts|Monk|XPHB|1|XPHB} die and has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute or until the target takes damage. While {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, the target takes the {@action Dash|XPHB} action and moves away from you by the safest route on each of its turns unless there is nowhere to move. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

subclassFeature.name: Primordial Patron
entries: Your pact draws on the Inner Planes, the realms of elemental forces and the building blocks of the multiverse. Warlocks of this pact act as heralds of ancient and destructive scions of air, earth, fire, and water—such as the Elemental Evils. These Warlocks prepare the way for their patrons' eventual arrival on the Material Plane by creating elemental nodes, areas suffused with energy from the Elemental Planes.

When you choose this subclass, choose an element from the {@table Primordial Patrons|XUA2026VillainousOptions2} table, which suggests potential patrons and determines the damage type for certain subclass features.
The capricious nature of elemental alliances extends to your pact. You can change your chosen element—and your patron—whenever you gain a level.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Node
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can create a 5-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} of elemental magic centered on a point you can see within 60 feet of yourself. The magic of this elemental node resembles your chosen element. On later turns, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to move the node up to 30 feet.

When the node appears, each creature other than you in the node makes a Dexterity saving throw against {@dcYourSpellSave}, taking {@damage 1d6} damage of your chosen element's type on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature also makes this save when the node moves into its space and when it enters the node or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

The node lasts for 1 minute, until you dismiss it (no action required), or until you use this feature to create another node. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you complete a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a {@classFeature Pact Magic|Warlock|XPHB|1|XPHB} spell slot (no action required) to restore your use of it.
The node's damage increases by {@damage 1d6} when you reach Warlock levels 6 ({@damage 2d6}) and 14 ({@damage 3d6}).

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Spells
entries: The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a {@class Warlock|XPHB} level specified in the {@table Elemental Spells|XUA2026VillainousOptions2} table, you thereafter always have the listed primordial spells prepared, along with the spells corresponding to your chosen element.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Haven
entries: Your {@subclassFeature Elemental Node|Warlock|XPHB|Primordial|XUA2026VillainousOptions2|3|XUA2026VillainousOptions2} shields you from harm. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Elemental Protection
entries: While you're within your node, you have a bonus to AC equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

entries.name: Elemental Teleport
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can {@variantrule Teleportation|XPHB|teleport} into your node or the nearest unoccupied space within 5 feet of it. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Primeval Protection
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Elemental Fortitude
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to your chosen element's damage type. Additionally, while within your {@subclassFeature Elemental Node|Warlock|XPHB|Primordial|XUA2026VillainousOptions2|3|XUA2026VillainousOptions2}, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to that damage type.

entries.name: Node Improvement
entries: Your {@subclassFeature Elemental Node|Warlock|XPHB|Primordial|XUA2026VillainousOptions2|3|XUA2026VillainousOptions2} is now a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Harbinger
entries: Your {@subclassFeature Elemental Node|Warlock|XPHB|Primordial|XUA2026VillainousOptions2|3|XUA2026VillainousOptions2} can usher in the mightiest of elementals, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Elemental Vortex
entries: Whenever you expend a {@classFeature Pact Magic|Warlock|XPHB|1|XPHB} spell slot while you're within your {@subclassFeature Elemental Node|Warlock|XPHB|Primordial|XUA2026VillainousOptions2|3|XUA2026VillainousOptions2}, you can attempt to pull a creature into the node. One creature you choose within 30 feet of the node must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 15 feet toward the node's center.

entries.name: Node Improvement
entries: Your {@subclassFeature Elemental Node|Warlock|XPHB|Primordial|XUA2026VillainousOptions2|3|XUA2026VillainousOptions2} now lasts for up to 1 hour.

entries.name: Primordial Herald
entries: While you're within your node's area, you can cast the {@spell Planar Ally|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot. When you cast the spell in this way, you speak the name of your patron. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish {@dice 2d4} {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Long Rests}.

optionalfeature.name: Elemental Overflow
entries: Choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. Whenever you cast a spell that deals the chosen damage type, you can cause elemental energy to wreathe you until the end of your next turn. For the duration, whenever a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack roll, that creature takes {@damage 1d4} damage of the chosen damage type.

entries.name: Repeatable
entries: You can gain this invocation more than once. Each time you do so, choose a different damage type.

optionalfeature.name: Elemental Transmutation
entries: Choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. Once per turn, whenever you deal damage of any of the above types, you can deal the chosen damage type instead.

table.name: Elemental Spells
rows: {@spell Summon Elemental|XPHB} (the spirit's element matches your chosen element)

table.name: Path of Lament Origins
colLabels: Your Rage Stems from the Time...
rows: A lost loved one rose as an undead monster.
You (and you alone) escaped brutal captors.
You found your family's charred remains.
Your decision led to a devastating shipwreck.
Your elders banished you from your home.

data.name: Unearthed Arcana 2026: Villainous Options 2
entries: This playtest document is part of a series of {@i Unearthed Arcana} articles that present material designed for upcoming products. The material here uses the rules in the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB}.

entries.name: What's Inside
entries: This document presents four new subclasses:

entries.name: This Is Playtest Material
entries: This article is presented for playtesting and feedback. The options here are experimental and in draft form. They aren't officially part of the game. Your feedback will help determine whether we adopt it as official.

items.name: How to Playtest This UA
entries: We invite you to try out this material in play. To play with this material, you may either incorporate it into your campaign or run one or more special playtest sessions. For such a session, you may create an adventure of your own or use a short adventure from a source like {@adventure Dragon Delves|DrDe-BD}.

items.name: Power Level
entries: The character options you read here might be more or less powerful than options in the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB}. If a design survives playtesting, we adjust its power to the desirable level before publication. This means an option could be more or less powerful in its final form.

items.name: Feedback
entries: The best way for you to give us feedback on this material is in the survey we'll release on D&D Beyond. If we make this material official, it will be refined based on your feedback, and then it will appear in a D&D book.
Providing feedback on this document is one way you can help shape the future of D&D!

entries: {@i Transform Bitter Anguish into Supernatural Rage}

entries: {@i Channel the Cauldron of Your Own Toxicity}

entries.name: Primordial Patron (Warlock)
entries: {@i Invoke Scions of Elemental Chaos}

entries.name: Eldritch Invocations
entries: The following two Eldritch Invocations complement the {@class Warlock|XPHB|Primordial Patron|Primordial|XUA2026VillainousOptions2}.
